name: Colorado River Below Glenwood Springs, CO
tempRationale: Direct Colorado River gauge at Glenwood should be treated as the truth source for this point
suitability: Colorado mainstem monitoring station below Glenwood Springs
note: USGS 09085100 sits below Glenwood Springs and is a strong live flow reference point on the Colorado.

name: Colorado R Abv Roaring Fork R at Glenwood Spgs, CO
tempRationale: Colorado gauge above the Roaring Fork confluence should be treated as a direct observed source for this confluence reference point
suitability: Colorado mainstem monitoring station above the Roaring Fork confluence
note: USGS 393259107194801 sits on the Colorado just above the Roaring Fork at Glenwood Springs and is a useful confluence reference point.

name: Colorado River Above Glenwood Springs, CO
tempRationale: Colorado gauge above Glenwood Springs should be treated as a direct observed temperature source for the upper Glenwood mainstem
suitability: Colorado mainstem monitoring station above Glenwood Springs
note: USGS 09071750 sits on the Colorado above Glenwood Springs and provides another upstream mainstem reference point.

name: Colorado R Blw South Canyon CR NR Glenwood Spgs CO
tempRationale: South Canyon downstream gauge should be treated as a direct observed temperature source for this reach
suitability: Colorado mainstem monitoring station below South Canyon Creek
note: USGS 09085160 sits on the Colorado below South Canyon Creek near Glenwood Springs and is a useful South Canyon reach reference point.

name: Grizzly Creek NR Grizzly Lake NR Glenwood Spgs, CO
tempRationale: Grizzly Creek gauge should be treated as a direct observed tributary flow source for Glenwood Canyon runoff context
suitability: High-elevation Grizzly Creek monitoring station above Glenwood Canyon
note: USGS 09071295 sits on upper Grizzly Creek and is a small tributary truth source for Glenwood Canyon runoff contribution.

name: Roaring Fork River at Glenwood Springs, Co.
tempRationale: Direct Roaring Fork gauge at Glenwood should be treated as the truth source for this point
suitability: Live Glenwood Roaring Fork monitoring station and river reference point
note: USGS 09085000 sits near the mouth of the Roaring Fork at Glenwood Springs and reports live flow temperature and turbidity.

name: CO River near dotsero
tempRationale: Dotsero gauge should be treated as the direct upstream Colorado truth source
suitability: Colorado mainstem monitoring station near Dotsero
note: USGS 09070500 is a Colorado River monitoring reference near Dotsero.

name: Fryingpan River Near Ruedi, Co.
tempRationale: Ruedi tailwater gauge should use direct observed temperature whenever available
suitability: Technical tailwater pocket water and seam fishing
note: USGS 09080400 sits just below Ruedi Dam and works well as an upper Fryingpan reference point.

name: Ruedi Reservoir
tempRationale: Ruedi Reservoir point is a reservoir-level monitoring location driven by Bureau of Reclamation pool elevation data rather than a standard river gauge
suitability: Current reservoir pool level and storage context
note: Ruedi Reservoir monitoring point using Bureau of Reclamation pool elevation data.

name: Rifle Gap Reservoir Near Rifle, CO
tempRationale: Rifle Gap Reservoir point is a reservoir-level monitoring location driven by USGS lake elevation and storage data rather than a standard river gauge
suitability: Current reservoir pool level and storage context
note: Rifle Gap Reservoir monitoring point using USGS lake elevation and storage data.

name: Roaring Fork River Near Aspen, CO
tempRationale: Aspen gauge should be treated as the upper-basin Roaring Fork truth source for observed flow
suitability: Upper Roaring Fork monitoring station above Aspen
note: USGS 09073400 is an upper-basin Roaring Fork truth source near Aspen with live discharge data but limited companion water-quality signals.

name: Roaring Fork River Blw Maroon Creek NR Aspen, CO
tempRationale: Maroon Creek Aspen gauge should be treated as the stronger upper-Fork truth source below Aspen because it carries live discharge and seasonal temperature coverage
suitability: Roaring Fork monitoring station below Maroon Creek near Aspen
note: USGS 09076300 is a stronger upper-Fork truth source below Maroon Creek with live discharge and seasonal water temperature coverage.

name: Roaring Fork River Near Emma, CO
tempRationale: Emma gauge should be the upstream truth source for the mid-valley Roaring Fork
suitability: Mid-valley Roaring Fork seam water and classic wade runs
note: USGS 09081000 is a good Emma-area reference point for the Roaring Fork.

name: Crystal River Abv Avalanche Crk, Near Redstone, CO
tempRationale: Upper Crystal gauge should use direct observed temperature whenever available
suitability: Upper Crystal freestone pockets and clear seam water
note: USGS 09081600 is a good Redstone-area monitoring reference on the upper Crystal River.

name: Colorado River Near Cameo, Co.
tempRationale: Cameo gauge should be the downstream Colorado truth source for long lower-river estimates
suitability: Lower Colorado monitoring station and big-river reference point
note: USGS 09095500 is a downstream Colorado River monitoring reference near Cameo.

name: Elk Creek at New Castle, Co.
tempRationale: Elk Creek at New Castle should be treated as a direct observed tributary temperature source where live temperature is available
suitability: Elk Creek monitoring station near New Castle and tributary reference point
note: USGS 09087500 sits on Elk Creek at New Castle and is a useful tributary monitoring reference west of Glenwood.

name: Parachute Creek at Parachute, Co.
tempRationale: Parachute Creek at Parachute should be treated as a direct observed tributary source where live data is available
suitability: Parachute Creek monitoring station and lower-valley tributary reference point
note: USGS 09093500 sits at Parachute and is a useful tributary monitoring reference west of Glenwood.

name: Roan Creek at DE Beque, Co.
tempRationale: Roan Creek at De Beque should be treated as a direct observed tributary source because it carries live flow temperature and turbidity
suitability: Roan Creek monitoring station near De Beque and lower-valley tributary reference point
note: USGS 391953108130201 sits on Roan Creek at De Beque and is a strong tributary truth source with live flow temperature and turbidity.
